owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) — CC gates writes; Akien holds the ruling.
question: Are the three roots SHELVES (each record belongs to one, decided by a placement test) or STATIONS on one road (private particular -> de-instanced pattern -> gated identity, where the question is not where a record belongs but how far along it is)? And if stations: what MOVES a record from one to the next?
why_it_is_load_bearing:
Four different partition rules were proposed in a single afternoon (2026-07-22) and each one re-derived the last: 'if losing it loses knowledge, it's commons' (toothless — it can never say no); 'would a co-developer need it' (Akien); 'would a co-developer re-derive it' (CC, Law 1 as a test); 'nothing only in CairnCommons that the system could not start without' (Akien). Then the two-layer DB conversation reframed all four as stations rather than shelves. That churn is the signal: the underlying SHAPE is unsettled, so every rule anchored to it is provisional.

Akien, 2026-07-22, on why this must not be rushed: 'the law is based on the shape. We are contemplating changing the shape. We are not changing the law like a trump follower, we are contemplating changing it because we see a need to change the underlying shape, which we are still contemplating. So we would need a new invariant to anchor the other things to, to be sure. I'm just not sure we've uncovered what it should be yet.'
reds: TRANSITION MECHANISM UNNAMED. If the roots are stations, something must move a record along the road — a ticket? a gate? a projector? nothing, and it is just re-filing? That mechanism IS the model; without it the road is a metaphor with no physics (Law 4).

EXHAUST IS NOT A STATION. The road model treats ~/.cairn as station one, 'private particular awaiting de-instancing.' But ~/.cairn also holds logs, caches, and credentials that are never on the road at all and never will be — waste, not pre-pattern. So instance-space contains at least two kinds of thing and the model currently conflates them. Either the road needs an off-road category, or station one is narrower than 'everything private.'

'WHAT CAN BE GATED vs WHAT CAN ONLY BE CITED' IS A PROMISE, NOT A MEASUREMENT. CC's candidate anchor — cairn/ is what the tester can fail on, CairnCommons is what a proof merely cites — is mechanically checkable in principle (scan the proofs for their inputs) but the tester is not built. Until it is, 'what can be gated' means 'what we intend to gate,' which is the same soft-test failure that made 'if losing it loses knowledge' useless.
what_would_clear_it: An invariant that (1) names the transition mechanism, (2) accounts for exhaust, and (3) is checkable by something that exists today rather than by the unbuilt tester. It must also EXPLAIN the core-values anomaly below rather than merely tolerate it — a rule that leaves our one known self-contradiction standing has not earned the churn of adopting it.

evidence_so_far.the_anomaly_that_is_the_best_probe:
`cairn/base/proofs/test_core_values.py` is a gate in cairn/ whose INPUT lives in CairnCommons (`intentions-other/core-values.md`), and its drift check ABSTAINS when that input is missing — it prints a NOTE and still returns green, so it cannot distinguish 'absent' from 'fine.' That is not sloppiness; it is the gate being honest about an impossible position, having been asked to prove something about a file it does not own. It is the one place the current shape already contradicts itself, which makes it the sharpest probe we have into what the anchor should be.

RULED (Akien, 2026-07-22): do NOT harden it yet. The abstain is anchored to a shape under active re-derivation, and re-pinning waits on the decision. CC's revised position: don't fix it, READ it — it is evidence, not a defect to clear.
evidence_so_far.the_collision_check: The road framing appears to collide with CLAUDE.md's three-roots table, which is written as shelves (Root | Holds | Rule). Checked by asking the why of each side: the table answers 'where do I put this' — a placement question; the road answers 'how does knowledge mature' — a lifecycle question. Not exclusive, not terminal. So this is a DISTINCTION TO RECORD, not a conflict; the table's Holds column stays true as a description of what rests at each station.
evidence_so_far.candidate_falsifier_for_the_road: Take ten existing records across all three roots. Two people place each on the road independently. Divergence on any one falsifies the model as a usable test — the same bar the seed test has to clear in `ticket-and-task`.
evidence_so_far.what_the_road_already_predicts_correctly: In-flight tickets stage in CairnCommons and migrate beside code as `history` when they prove — which is exactly the moment a proof starts depending on them. The road reproduces a migration rule we already run rather than contradicting it. That is the strongest evidence for it so far, and it is n=1.
evidence_so_far.the_startability_invariant_it_must_absor: Akien, 2026-07-22: 'nothing is only in CairnCommons that the system could not start without. A brand new user can use our CairnCommons or build their own — else why bother having two repos at all?' This is the best-tested candidate: it saves CairnCommons rather than dissolving it, by giving it the job of CORPUS (what this lineage compiled) against cairn/ as ENGINE. Open sub-question CC raised and Akien has not ruled on: START or SUSTAIN? A fresh box starts fine without the host-seam re-verify driver and rots without it.

measured: CairnCommons is 388K, 43 commits, 7 directories; 48 files carry CairnCommons/ path references (27 in cairn/, 21 internal). Whatever the answer, the move is cheap today and gets more expensive every day (2026-07-22).
blocks: ticket-and-task child (a) — where a task berths
any move of core-values.md, telos.md, or the intentions seed
the seed-DB layering (which layer lives where)
